name: Interior Archetype - Shop
required_conditions: The interior is a single room, not a multi-room shop, shop-plus-back-office, or shop-plus-living-quarters layout.
The interior has exactly one primary entrance/exit shared by both directions of travel.
The interior's commercial function is retail browsing, not a market stall, auction hall, or crafting workshop.
The interior is reached from a town or village exterior map, not from a dungeon or wilderness screen.

provenance.notes: Confidence inherited directly from the source pattern: corroborated by exactly one official sample map (Map021.json). Do not report this archetype as High confidence in a citing implementation contract. WO-0059 candidate not authored: a zoned/expanded (shop-plus-back-office) layout tier was considered and explicitly rejected, not merely omitted -- PAT-INTERIOR-SHOP's Required Conditions state the interior must be 'a single room, not a multi-room shop, shop-plus-back-office, or shop-plus-living-quarters layout', and WO-0059's own constraints forbid loosening an accepted pattern's requirements without an explicit proposed exception. Building a zoned/expanded ARCH-INTERIOR-SHOP layout family would violate that condition outright, not just carry low confidence. If a back-office shop is ever wanted, it needs its own proposed exception against PAT-INTERIOR-SHOP (or a new archetype), not a LayoutFamily that quietly ignores the archetype's own required_conditions.
